---
title: "Document Title"
subtitle: "A short descriptive subtitle"
author: "Author Name, Role"
date: today
format: probity-typst
lang: en-GB
# Running header/footer text (all optional):
header-text: "Data Analytics"          # right of the logo (omit → default "Data Analytics")
footer-text: "Probity Data Analytics"  # bold navy brand at the foot, left (omit → default)
footer-note: "Confidential"            # muted classification note; remove this line to show no note
abstract: |
  One short paragraph stating what this document is and what it concludes.
  Lead with the answer, then the qualification.
---

# Section Heading

Body text in the Probity register: plain, direct, UK spelling, no em dashes.
Lead with the answer, then state the dependency or limitation in the next
sentence. Keep one idea per sentence.

## Subsection

A **bold navy lead-in.** Then the body text continues, as used heavily in
caveat lists.

- First point
- Second point
- Third point

### Smaller Heading

A table renders with a navy header row and alternating body rows:

| Variant | Description | Value |
|---|---|---|
| S0 | Baseline | R 14,903,239 |
| S1 | Drop COVID year | R 12,410,008 |
| S2 | Lag-1 GDP | R 13,775,512 |

: Provision under each scenario {#tbl-scenarios}

A figure with a caption:

![Loss rate against lag-1 GDP](_extensions/probity/assets/logo_trim.png){#fig-fit width=60%}

> A blockquote, useful for the honesty pattern: state the headline finding,
> then state what it depends on.

Inline `code` and a fenced block:

```python
import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv("data.csv")
print(df.head())
```
